title: Bug: locking contention between create and ps -a
body:
It looks like there is a locking issue between podman create and ps -a where both operations get hung waiting on a lock.  You can replicate this by...

in one terminal run:
```
while true; do clear && sudo podman ps -a && sleep .5; done
```
And in another do:
```
for i in `seq 0 100`; do sudo podman create alpine ls; sudo podman create alpine ls; sudo podman rm -l; done
```
After a short time, both sessions will lock...stopping one of them, the other will continue

comments.body:
@mheon im running a heavily debug'd version of podman and I am observing some interesting results.
Consider the following from podman create
```
New create is beginning
called from /home/bbaude/git/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/store.go#505 -> /var/lib/containers/storage/storage.lock
called from /home/bbaude/git/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/store.go#659
called from /home/bbaude/git/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/layers.go#380 -> /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-layers/layers.lock
called from /home/bbaude/git/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/store.go#577
called from /home/bbaude/git/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/images.go#224 -> /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-images/images.lock
called from /home/bbaude/git/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/store.go#590
called from /home/bbaude/git/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/containers.go#190 -> /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/containers.lock
called from /home/bbaude/git/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/libpod/runtime.go#256 -> /var/run/libpod/alive.lck
--->
called from /home/bbaude/git/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/libpod/container_internal.go#163 -> /var/run/libpod/lock/fc075d331de79ec0365252342917891f7f5d41f94ece0fd55454a97e7e824014
fc075d331de79ec0365252342917891f7f5d41f94ece0fd55454a97e7e824014
```

All of the lock calls above the --> are coming from the getruntime in the create command.  Now lets look at ps -a.
```
± |locks U:6 ?:15 ✗| → sudo podman ps -a
called from /home/bbaude/git/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/store.go#505 -> /var/lib/containers/storage/storage.lock
called from /home/bbaude/git/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/store.go#659
called from /home/bbaude/git/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/layers.go#380 -> /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-layers/layers.lock
called from /home/bbaude/git/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/store.go#577
called from /home/bbaude/git/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/images.go#224 -> /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-images/images.lock
called from /home/bbaude/git/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/store.go#590
called from /home/bbaude/git/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/containers.go#190 -> /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/containers.lock
called from /home/bbaude/git/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/libpod/runtime.go#256 -> /var/run/libpod/alive.lck
--->
called from /home/bbaude/git/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/libpod/boltdb_state_internal.go#241 -> /var/run/libpod/lock/fe7c85a08eca29bb7b2566918af90d30c390491f67cddca750cf43c7418947ea
CONTAINER ID   IMAGE    COMMAND   CREATED AT                      STATUS    PORTS   NAMES
fe7c85a08eca   alpine   ls        2018-03-02 19:58:48 -0600 CST   Created           sleepy_wiles
```
Here again, all the locks are coming prior to getRuntime.  And when these two go into lock contention, it is over this lock:

```
New create is beginning
called from /home/bbaude/git/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/store.go#505 -> /var/lib/containers/storage/storage.lock
called from /home/bbaude/git/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/store.go#659
called from /home/bbaude/git/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/_output/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/layers.go#380 -> /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-layers/layers.lock
```

So I'm really now thinking this lock contention is a result of getruntime.

It might be store initialization, then? We don't do much else with c/storage in NewRuntime

If so that explains why CRI-O hasn't seen this in their perf testing - they only need to run store init once, when the daemon starts up.
is storage initialization done in cs?

@baude I'm thinking specifically about https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/blob/master/libpod/runtime.go#L137

That's our only significant interaction with c/storage in NewRuntime. It can either retrieve an existing store or make a new one, which leads me to believe it's probably fairly heavyweight?
Well this can occur with two different terminals creating containers too ...

If it actually is runtime creation, we can lock runtime creation. We already lock a (small) part of it, so extending it to the entire runtime would not be hard. It wouldn't really cost us much in the way of performance single-threaded either, since we already make the lock and lock/unlock it, we'd just be locking more.

The big hit would be starting multiple podman processes concurrently. I don't know if that is a perf hit we really care about. CRI-O uses would not be affected at all, given they only call NewRuntime once.

Of course, this doesn't address the actual race in c/storage. We should try and characterize that and report it upstream.
